description: ContractorDesk state-specific home improvement contract templates — ALL 50 states + DC + Federal

AL.statute: Alabama Code Title 34, Chapter 8 (General Contractors); Ala. Code 34-14A (Home Builders Licensure Board)

AR.statute: Arkansas Code Title 17, Chapter 25 (Contractors Licensing Board)

AZ.license_label: Arizona Registrar of Contractors (ROC) License Number
AZ.statute: Arizona Revised Statutes § 32-1101 et seq. (Registrar of Contractors); A.R.S. § 33-981 (Mechanics Lien Law)

CO.statute: No statewide GC license law; local municipalities govern. C.R.S. § 12-58 (plumbing/electrical licensing)

CT.license_label: CT Home Improvement Contractor Registration Number
CT.statute: Connecticut General Statutes § 20-418 et seq. (Home Improvement Act); § 20-417a et seq. (New Home Construction)

DC.license_label: DC Basic Business License Number (General Contractor or Home Improvement)
DC.statute: DC Code § 47-2881 et seq.; DC Municipal Regulations Title 16

DE.statute: No statewide GC license. Specialty trades licensed under Delaware Code Title 24

FEDERAL.statute: Truth in Lending Act (TILA), 15 U.S.C. § 1635; Regulation Z; Electronic Signatures in Global and National Commerce Act (

GA.statute: O.C.G.A. § 43-41-1 et seq.; Georgia Lien Law O.C.G.A. § 44-14-361

IA.statute: Iowa Code § 91C (Contractor Registration); Iowa Code § 572 (Mechanics Liens)

ID.statute: Idaho Code § 54-5201 et seq. (Contractors Board Registration)

IL.statute: Illinois Home Repair and Remodeling Act, 815 ILCS 513/1 et seq.

IN.statute: Local municipal ordinances govern most licensing. Ind. Code § 25-28.5 (plumbing licensing)

KS.statute: No statewide license law. Local county/city ordinances govern.

KY.statute: KRS § 198B (electrical/plumbing/HVAC state licenses); Local municipal ordinances for GC

LA.license_label: Louisiana State Licensing Board for Contractors (LSLBC) License Number
LA.statute: Louisiana Revised Statutes § 37:2150 et seq. (LSLBC); Home Improvement Contractors Act R.S. § 37:2167

MA.license_label: MA Home Improvement Contractor (HIC) Registration Number / Construction Supervisor License (CSL) Number
MA.statute: M.G.L. Chapter 142A (Home Improvement Contractor Registration); M.G.L. Chapter 149, § 44F (Construction Supervisor Licen

MD.statute: Maryland Code Business Regulation § 8-301 et seq. (Maryland Home Improvement Commission); MHIC

ME.statute: Mostly local municipal licensing. Title 32, chapter 73 (electrical); Title 32, chapter 17 (plumbing). Written contract r

MI.license_label: Michigan Residential Builders or Maintenance and Alteration Contractors License Number
MI.statute: Michigan Occupational Code MCL § 339.2401 et seq. (Residential Builders); MCL § 339.2601 (Maintenance and Alteration Con

MN.license_label: Minnesota Residential Building Contractor License Number

MO.statute: No statewide GC license law. Local municipal ordinances govern. Mo. Rev. Stat. § 327.400 (electrical)

MS.license_label: Mississippi State Board of Contractors License Number
MS.statute: Mississippi Code § 31-3-1 et seq. (Mississippi State Board of Contractors)

MT.statute: Montana Code Annotated § 39-9-101 et seq. (Construction Contractor Registration)

NC.statute: N.C.G.S. § 87-1 et seq. (General Contractor Licensing); N.C.G.S. § 44A (Mechanics Lien Law)

NE.statute: Nebraska Revised Statutes § 48-2101 et seq. (Contractor Registration); § 54-301 (Mechanics Lien)

NH.statute: RSA 319-C (electrical licensing); RSA 153:34 (plumbing licensing). Local ordinances govern GC.

NJ.license_label: NJ Home Improvement Contractor Registration Number
NJ.statute: New Jersey Statutes § 56:8-136 et seq. (Contractors' Registration Act); N.J.A.C. 13:45A (Consumer Affairs)

NM.license_label: New Mexico Construction Industries Division (CID) License Number
NM.statute: NMSA § 60-13-1 et seq. (Construction Industries Licensing Act); NMAC Title 14, Chapter 6

NV.license_label: Nevada State Contractors Board License Number
NV.statute: Nevada Revised Statutes Chapter 624 (Nevada State Contractors Board)

NY.license_label: NY Home Improvement Contractor License / Registration Number
NY.statute: New York General Business Law § 771; NYC Administrative Code § 20-387 through § 20-394

OH.statute: Ohio Revised Code § 4722 (Home Construction Services Suppliers Act); Updated by HB50 (2024)

OK.statute: Oklahoma Statutes Title 59, § 1000 et seq. (Construction Industries Board)

OR.license_label: Oregon Construction Contractors Board (CCB) License Number
OR.statute: Oregon Revised Statutes § 701.005 et seq. (Oregon Construction Contractors Board)

PA.license_label: PA Home Improvement Contractor Registration Number (HIC#)
PA.statute: Home Improvement Consumer Protection Act (HICPA), 73 P.S. § 517.1 et seq.

RI.statute: Rhode Island General Laws § 5-65 (Contractors' Registration and Licensing Board)

SC.license_label: SC General Contractor License Number / Residential Builder License Number
SC.statute: SC Code § 40-11-5 et seq. (SC Contractors Licensing Board); § 40-59 (Residential Builders Commission)

SD.license_label: South Dakota Contractor's Excise Tax License Number
SD.statute: SDCL § 10-46A (Contractors Excise Tax License); local municipal ordinances govern licensing

TN.statute: Tennessee Code § 62-6-101 et seq. (Board for Licensing Contractors); § 62-6-503 (Home Improvement Law)

TX.statute: Texas Property Code § 41.007, § 53.255; Texas Business & Commerce Code § 17.46 (DTPA)

UT.license_label: Utah Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing (DOPL) License Number
UT.statute: Utah Code § 58-55-301 et seq. (Utah Construction Trades Licensing Act)

VA.statute: Virginia Code § 54.1-1100 et seq. (Board for Contractors); § 54.1-1115 (penalties)

VT.statute: No statewide GC license. VSA Title 26 (electrical/plumbing licensing). Local ordinances govern GC.

WA.license_label: Washington State UBI Number (Unified Business Identifier) / Contractor Registration Number

WI.license_label: Wisconsin Dwelling Contractor Qualifier Certification Number

WY.statute: Wyoming Statutes § 35-9-128 (electrical licensing). Local municipal ordinances govern GC licensing.

usage_notes: Load the contractor's state from their profile (set during onboarding)
ALWAYS also load FEDERAL_template.json — it applies in every state
Auto-inject contractor license number from profile into {{license_number}} placeholders
Render cancellation deadline date dynamically: contract_date + cancellation_days_standard (count Saturdays for federal notice)
For CA: check if buyer is senior citizen (65+) → use 5-day version. Check if post-disaster declared emergency → use 7-day version.
For TX: include BOTH TX_HOME_SOLICITATION and FEDERAL_3DAY — two separate notices are legally required
For NC: check if project >= $30,000 — if so, lien agent designation is required before work begins
For TN: check if job is in Bradley, Davidson, Hamilton, Haywood, Knox, Marion, Robertson, Rutherford, or Shelby county AND project is $3,000-$25,000 — if so, Home Improvement License is required
For VA: mechanics lien must include license number AND expiration date — missing either invalidates the lien
For WA: GCs that hire subs must register as 'general contractor' (specialty contractors cannot legally subcontract)
For RI: GL insurance must list the CRLB as certificate holder (new 2024 requirement)
The GENERAL_DISCLAIMER ('not legal advice') must appear on every generated contract
States with NO statewide GC license (local only): CO, DE, IN, KS, KY, ME, MO, NH, SD, VT, WY
States requiring license for lien rights: AZ, GA, ID, MI, MN, MS, NM, NV, OR, RI, SC, TN (residential), WA
